outlook.volatilityExpectation: Low to moderate; heavily positive Total GEX suppresses volatility despite extreme Put/Call ratios.
outlook.summary: The market is trapped in a dealer-hedging stranglehold. Extremely positive Total GEX (4.40B in 0DTE alone) creates a stabilizing effect, pinning the price near the 0DTE Gamma Flip (678.73). Although Put/Call Ratios are heavily skewed towards puts (>2.0) and volatility smirks indicate strong bearish sentiment, dealer hedging dynamics will likely suppress volatility and magnetize the spot towards 0DTE Max Pain at 671.00. Watch the 680 Call Wall as the definitive upside cap.

Monumental Put OI in the Monthly expiration (129.8k), backed by 0DTE and Weekly builds. Represents the ultimate downside hedge barrier.
Strong Call OI across all expiries (14.3k 0DTE, 7.4k Weekly, 25.1k Monthly). Serves as a secondary resistance ceiling above 680.
Heavy Put OI concentration in 0DTE (34.0k) and Monthly (46.7k). Intermediate structural support backed by bearish sentiment.
Major Call OI in 0DTE (13.0k) and Monthly (33.9k). Final upside barrier for the current option series.
0DTE Max Pain level, located roughly 1.2% below the current spot. Positive GEX will naturally attract the price towards this level as expiry approaches.
